author: Sofia Martínez
title: Get Your Local Business Into Google AI Overviews (With Real Examples)
description: Google AI Overviews now show at the top of local searches. We decoded the pattern and got 8 service businesses cited in them.

sections.p: Google AI Overviews—those AI-generated summaries that appear at the top of search results—started appearing for local queries in 2025. When someone searches "best plumber in Portland" or "how to fix a burst pipe," Google's AI now pulls answers from top-ranking sites and cites them. Getting cited means clicks and authority. We tracked this for 6 months, reverse-engineered the ranking factors, and got cited in AI Overviews for 14 local queries across 8 businesses. Three businesses reported 18-24% higher click-through rates from search. Here's how we did it.

sections.h2: The AI Overview Citation Pattern: Question-Answer Format Wins

sections.p: AI Overviews cite sources that answer questions directly and comprehensively. When Google's model reads your content, it's looking for clear, structured answers to common questions in your industry. We analyzed 200+ local queries and the sites cited in their AI Overviews and found a clear pattern: pages with question-answer format, numbered steps, and data-backed claims got cited 3x more often than generic blog posts.

Example: a Denver HVAC company ranked on page 2 for "how much does a furnace replacement cost?" Their page was good—2,000 words, informative. But it wasn't cited in AI Overviews. We restructured it: started with a direct answer ("Furnace replacement in Denver costs $4,500-$8,200 depending on unit and installation"), followed by a breakdown (new furnace unit: $1,800-$4,000; labor: $1,500-$2,500; removal: $400-$800), then included local factors ("Denver homes average 2,000 sq ft requiring 80,000-100,000 BTU units"). Within 4 weeks, it got cited in the AI Overview for 4 related queries and their organic CTR jumped from 2.1% to 5.8%.

list: Start with a direct answer in the first paragraph (not a narrative intro)
Break complex answers into numbered or bulleted steps
Include local data: prices, timeframes, regulations specific to your region
Use table or list format for comparisons or costs
Cite sources or studies when you reference industry statistics
Keep answer chunks under 300 words each (AI prefers concise blocks)

sections.h2: Local Data and Authority: The AI Overview Multiplier

sections.p: AI Overviews prioritize sites that include authoritative, locally-specific data. Generic content gets deprioritized. We tested this theory: one law firm had a page titled "How Much Does a Divorce Cost?" with national averages. It wasn't cited. We updated it with a new version: "How Much Does a Divorce Cost in California?" with CA-specific filing fees ($309), custody evaluation costs ($1,500-$3,000), and references to California Family Code sections. Citations in AI Overviews increased from 0 to 3 within 6 weeks, and the page now ranks in top 3.

The mechanism: local data signals expertise to Google's model. When you cite your state's regulations, local market rates, or neighborhood-specific examples, you're telling the AI "I know this community." A physical therapist in Boston got cited in AI Overviews for "ACL injury recovery timeline" by adding: "In Massachusetts, physical therapy is often covered by insurance with a $30-50 copay, and most patients see improvement in 3-4 months." Generic competitor sites weren't cited. Local context wins.

sections.quote: We updated 6 pages with location-specific data and pricing. Within 8 weeks, we went from being cited in zero AI Overviews to being cited in 8. Our organic leads from search increased 22%.

sections.h2: Schema Markup and E-E-A-T: Making AI Trust You

sections.p: Google's AI considers E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) when deciding whose content to cite. Schema markup helps communicate this. We added LocalBusiness, FAQPage, and HowTo schema to pages targeting AI Overview visibility. A home inspection company added FAQPage schema to their "What to look for in a home inspection" page. It started getting cited within 3 weeks.

More important: establish author expertise. If your page about "roof leak repair costs" is written by an unnamed author, AI is skeptical. If it's written by "Mike Chen, 20-year roofing contractor, certified through NRCA," the AI is confident. Add author bios with credentials. Include your professional certifications, years in business, and relevant achievements. A dental practice added their dentist's DDS credential, specialization in cosmetic dentistry, and 15 years of practice to their author profile. Pages written by them started appearing in AI Overviews for cosmetic dental queries.

sections.h2: The Query Expansion Strategy: Get Cited for 10+ Related Queries

sections.p: One mistake: treating each AI Overview citation separately. Smart move: create content clusters. When you build comprehensive, interconnected content around a core topic, you get cited for variations. We built this for a plumbing company. Core topic: emergency plumber services. We created: 1) "Emergency Plumber Near Me: What to Expect" (pricing, response times, local regulations), 2) "Burst Pipe: Temporary Fix vs Professional Repair," 3) "Water Damage from Burst Pipes: Who Pays?" (links to local insurance info), 4) "24-Hour Emergency Plumber [City]: Our Response Protocol." These 4 pages internally linked heavily and shared schema markup. Result: the company got cited in AI Overviews for 9 different emergency plumbing queries (not just one). Total organic traffic to this cluster grew 41% in 12 weeks.

Build your cluster this way: choose 1 core topic your customers search for. Create 4-6 pages covering angles: cost, local regulations, how-to, timing, common mistakes. Make sure each page answers a different commonly-asked question. Interlink them. Use consistent schema. This signals to Google that you're a comprehensive authority, and AI Overviews cite you across multiple related queries instead of just one.

list: Map 8-12 related queries around your main topic
Create 4-6 satellite pages (1,200-1,800 words each, different angles)
Interlink with anchor text describing the destination page
Use FAQPage schema on the core page listing all questions
Update monthly with new data or local market changes
